meta.notes: numeric equality rejects scale 0 versus scale 2 inside captured-outer Having

diagnostics.summary: The maximum number of fractional digits of the left-hand side (0) and of the right-hand side (2) are different. [MVK_INVALID_COMPARE_DEC_PLACES]
diagnostics.fix: The two decimal operands here must share the SAME declared scale (fractional digits) — only == / != and a computed-field assignment are checked; ORDERING (< <= > >=) is exempt. TWO contexts trigger it: (1) a COMPUTED FIELD whose declared scale differs from its expression's operands (e.g. a field re-scaled to 2 places while the Sum-computed field stays scale 0) — re-scale the computed field (or the operands) so their fractional digits agree, doing both edits in ONE `apply` so the terminal gate sees a consistent model; (2) a field-vs-field == / != across differing scales — either round one side to the other's scale with RoundDown(expr, n), or accept the mismatch with allowDifferingDecimals:true on the rule spec (the kernel's one suppressible warning — it prepends @SuppressWarning(MVK_INVALID_COMPARE_DEC_PLACES)).
diagnostics.explain: MVK_INVALID_COMPARE_DEC_PLACES ("the fractional digits of lhs and rhs are different") fires wherever the kernel compares two decimal operands of unequal declared scale — an == / != field comparison OR a computed field whose scale doesn't match its expression. It is not specific to any one operator (Sum, Equal, …); the scale-coupling is the constraint, so the corrective is context-dependent.
